title: SpecialtyRouting
description: Study -> subspecialty routing for critical-result on-call paging (#58). Rules are evaluated in order, first match wins; a matched study's on-call directory search is narrowed to that specialty, and outOfSpecialtyFallback decides the failure direction when nobody in it is on call. Config only -- modalities, keywords, specialty tokens; never PHI. Lives beside the Communications Agent (agents/communications/specialty-routing.yaml).

outOfSpecialtyFallback.description: When a specialty is derived but nobody tagged for it is on call: `any-on-call` pages whoever is on call and stamps the Communication out-of-specialty; `none` pages nobody from the agent -- the dispatch reports the miss honestly (SKIPPED / escalated:false) and the workflow archives it, with NO further automatic paging (the #29 sign-off ladder runs pre-sign and does not react to dispatch outcomes). These fail in opposite directions -- a safety call, not an implementation detail.

rules.description: Evaluated in order; FIRST match wins. Each rule needs at least one of modalities/keywords non-empty -- a rule that can never match is a config error (enforced by validate_contracts.py).

specialty.description: The specialty token the deployment tags its on-call PractitionerRoles with (ledger PractitionerRole.specialty coding code; pushed as the FHIR `specialty` search param).

modalities.description: DICOM modality codes matched exactly, case-insensitive (e.g. MG).
